canvas.safeZoneLabel: Right third reserved for Nine camera

voiceover: Start with the first four digits you will hear all the time: ศูนย์ sǔun, หนึ่ง nùeng, สอง sǎawng, สาม sǎam.
For room numbers, phone numbers, and queue numbers, Thai usually says each digit one by one, not as one big math word.
One important beginner note: หนึ่ง often gets reduced in faster Thai later, but today we keep the full careful form so your ear has something solid to hold on to.
overlays: Build the first digit map: 0 to 3
Show the first four digits as a clean memory set with large Thai script and Arabic numerals grouped together on the left.
scenes.teachingObjective: Give the learner a clean starting map for zero through three and explain that everyday Thai codes are usually said digit by digit.

visualStrategy.onScreenGoal: Show the first four digits as a clean memory set with large Thai script and Arabic numerals grouped together on the left.
teachingVisuals: Large stacked cards for 0-3 with Thai script above the transliteration.
A simple line showing digit-by-digit reading for a code like 1-0-3.
teacherCues: Pause after each digit and keep the learner on one sound at a time.
Point to the Arabic numeral only after the learner has seen the Thai script and heard the spoken form.
visualStrategy.rationale: The digits themselves are the teaching target here, so a real-world image would distract more than it would help.

assets.query: M01-L004 Numbers 0 to 10: Age, Room Numbers, and Basic Counting Build the first digit map: 0 to 3 thai learning visual
assets.license: Noun Project search result review required
assets.usageNotes: Text-first scene. Optional icon fallback only if the layout needs a minimal anchor.

voiceover: Now add the middle set: สี่ sìi, ห้า hâa, หก hòk, เจ็ด jèt.
Do not treat these as just English labels with Thai written next to them. Hear the shape of each word: สี่ is long, หก is clipped, and เจ็ด ends sharply.
If you stretch หก or soften the ending in เจ็ด too much, you will sound less natural and your listening will stay fuzzy.
overlays: Contrast the sound-shapes of 4-7 so the learner can see long versus clipped forms without crowding the screen.
scenes.teachingObjective: Teach the middle set where beginners often blur vowels or rush final consonants, especially with สี่, ห้า, หก, and เจ็ด.

visualStrategy.onScreenGoal: Contrast the sound-shapes of 4-7 so the learner can see long versus clipped forms without crowding the screen.
teachingVisuals: Two-column comparison board pairing long shapes and short clipped shapes.
A rhythm bar under each digit showing whether the learner should hold or cut the sound.
teacherCues: Model the digits once naturally and once slowly.
Use hand timing to show that หก and เจ็ด finish earlier than สี่ and ห้า.
visualStrategy.rationale: This section is about pronunciation timing and contrast, so graphic timing cues teach better than stock imagery.

assets.query: M01-L004 Numbers 0 to 10: Age, Room Numbers, and Basic Counting Add 4 to 7 with pronunciation control thai learning visual
assets.license: Noun Project search result review required
assets.usageNotes: Text-first scene. Optional icon fallback only if the layout needs a minimal anchor.

voiceover: Finish the set with แปด bpàaet, เก้า gâao, and สิบ sìp.
สิบ means ten, but for now the real job is not mental arithmetic. The real job is hearing each digit clearly and saying it back clearly.
Then add the chunk เบอร์อะไร ber à-rai. เบอร์ is a very practical borrowed word for a number label, so you hear it with rooms, phones, tables, and tickets.
overlays: Finish 8 to 10 and ask for a number
Anchor the digit set in a real-world number context so the learner sees why saying each digit separately matters.
scenes.teachingObjective: Complete the 0-10 set and introduce เบอร์อะไร as a practical chunk for asking about room numbers, table numbers, and other everyday number labels.

visualStrategy.onScreenGoal: Anchor the digit set in a real-world number context so the learner sees why saying each digit separately matters.
teachingVisuals: Large room-number example such as 5-0-2 with each Thai digit spoken separately underneath.
Short prompt card showing เบอร์อะไร on the left and a three-digit answer on the next beat.
teacherCues: Keep the room-number example large and leave the right third empty for Nine.
Do not let the photo dominate the scene; use it only as a context anchor behind the left teaching card.
visualStrategy.rationale: A real room-number plaque or hotel corridor sign makes the use case instantly concrete and helps the learner understand why Thai digits are often said one by one.

assets.query: Thailand hotel room number door plaque
assets.usageNotes: Use as left-panel supporting visual only; keep text dominant.

voiceover: Numbers become much more useful when they plug into the people language you already know. From lesson three, you can now drop digits into frames such as ฉันอายุสิบปี or เขาอายุเก้าปี.
You already know pronouns like ฉัน, ผม, คุณ, and เขา. That means numbers are no longer floating on their own; they attach to real people, room numbers, and simple personal facts.
If a number comes too fast, fall back on the repair phrases from the first foundation lesson, such as ขอโทษ khǎaw-thôot, then ask again and keep the number exchange moving.
overlays: Use numbers in short real phrases
Show numbers dropping into pronoun-led phrases so the learner sees age and room-number language as part of the same first-contact toolkit.
ฉันอายุสิบปี | chǎn aa-yú sìp bpii | I am ten years old
scenes.teachingObjective: Move from isolated digits into usable age and room-number phrases while explicitly reusing the pronoun and identity material from the rebuilt foundation lessons.

visualStrategy.onScreenGoal: Show numbers dropping into pronoun-led phrases so the learner sees age and room-number language as part of the same first-contact toolkit.
teachingVisuals: A compact phrase ladder: ฉัน -> อายุ -> สิบ -> ปี, then a second line with เขาอายุเก้าปี.
A repair-and-repeat mini-dialogue showing ขอโทษ before the room number is repeated clearly.
teacherCues: Tie the age pattern explicitly back to the pronouns from the previous lesson.
Keep the room-number question and answer separate from the age line so the learner hears two different number jobs.
visualStrategy.rationale: This section is about plugging digits into phrase frames, so a clean ladder teaches better than another contextual photo.

เขาอายุเก้าปี
